themes.exampleQuote: They are made in house and are liege waffles. So, more chewy and sugary with bits of crunch at the edges.
waffle with chocolate ice cream and nutella is the best.
Awesome neighborhood place to take the kids. Consistently great waffles!
best waffles ive ever had and the best ice cream in the city.
a great walking date stop on a cold day. we can pop in, grab a sugar waffle and eat it piping hot while walking.

notable_quote: When the two are paired together, it's just exquisite.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers are consistently floored by the Liège waffle's chew and caramelized edges, and they single out the ice cream as a genuine standout; the one recurring note is that Nutella flavor in the ice cream could hit harder.
quarter_narrative: Twenty-eight creators filmed Waffallonia this quarter and collectively pulled nearly seven million TikTok plays. Waffallonia posted none of it back — no TikTok account, an Instagram that went dark at an unknown point, and a profile that isn't even registered as a business account. The city is doing the work; the shop is watching from the sidewalk. Meanwhile, Page's is leading the Pittsburgh Sweets family on social momentum, which is exactly the axis where Waffallonia is bleeding rank despite holding the strongest review signal and a perfect conversion path in its class. What keeps Waffallonia at #1 in Dessert restaurants is a 605-review foundation and creator goodwill it has not yet had to earn — what moves it up the Sweets family ranking is whether it eventually shows up in its own feed.
tldr_read: Nearly seven million TikTok plays from 28 creators, a dormant Instagram, and zero presence on its own social channels — Icons of the Burgh, #1 in Pittsburgh Dessert restaurants.
tldr_meaning: The review and discovery signals are already at the top of the family; the only axis suppressing the overall rank is an Instagram momentum reading well below the family median, and that gap widens every week the account stays quiet.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: Seven Million TikTok Plays, One Business Not Watching Its Own Feed.

playbook.headline: Restart the Instagram account, register it properly
playbook.action: Convert @waffallonia to a business account, add a bio, and post once — the account has no bio, no business registration, and an unknown last post date.

playbook.headline: Claim the TikTok footage already in the wild
playbook.action: Open a business TikTok and repost or stitch the 28 creators who drove 6.9M plays this quarter — starting with @katelynnsnuggle's 2.1M-play video.

playbook.headline: Expand Google photo categories with video
playbook.action: Add short in-house video clips to Google — the 291 existing photos are strong but the Videos category is underpopulated relative to the Food & drink and Vibe sets.
